name: Aquatainer 10L Collapsible
description: The Aquatainer 10 L Collapsible is a collapsible 10 L water container with a tap and handle for carrying and storing water.

taxonomy.sub_category: Aquatainer 10L Collapsible Updated on January 10, 2024 · Created on August 15, 2016 Aquatainer 10L Collapsible The Aquatainer 10 L Collapsible is a collapsible 10 L water container with a tap and handle for carrying and storing water. Developed By NRSRelief Tested By The International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies (IFRC) United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description The Aquatainer 10 L Collapsible is a UV-stabilized and impact-resistant safe water container manufactured from food-grade low-density polyethylene without toxic elements. It comes with or without a spigot (or faucet). Target SDGs SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation SDG 3: Good Health and Well-Being Market Suggested Retail Price $2.00 Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household Distributors / Implementing Organizations NRS Relief Competitive Landscape Direct competitors include Oxfam Bucket and CDC SWS Container . Regions Africa, Asia, Middle East Manufacturing/Building Method This product is manufactured by NRS Relief at their production facility, H. Sheikh Noor-ud-Din & Sons, in Lahore, Pakistan. Intellectural Property Type Trade Secret User Provision Model NRS Relief is a product supplier to aid organizations. Organizations and individuals can also contact NRS Relief directly for quotes and procurement. Distributions to Date Status Between 2017 to 2020, it is estimated that over 100,000 water containers have been distributed by NRS Relief. Interview with Representatives in 2020 Container volume (L) 10 L Integrated dispensing tap (yes/no) Yes Handle for carrying (yes/no) Yes Material Low-density polyethylene (LDPE) Design Specifications The Aquatainer 10 L Collapsible is manufactured with food-grade LDPE (low-density polyethylene) and fitted with a built-in carrying handle and a removable cap for filling and discharge. It can withstand temperatures between -20 °C and +50 °C. It is impact-resistant, translucent, and UV-stabilized. Dimensions are as follows: Capacity: 10 L Weight: 180 – 230 g Average thickness: Minimum 30 mm Built-in handle: 90 x 30 mm Polyamide string: 1 mm diameter and 120 mm long Product Schematics Technical Support Technical support is provided by the manufacturer. Replacement Components Not available Lifecycle Unknown Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters Designer specified performance targets include: meeting ICRC/IFRC standards, portability and storage of clean drinking water, UV-stabilized, and impact-resistant. Vetted Performance Status Testing performed by the manufacturer to determine the product’s impact resistance through impact resistance/drop tests from a 2 m height without damage or leakage. To be accepted, the container must withstand a minimum of 3 drops. Additionally, the product is tested in independent laboratories for compliance with UNHCR and ICRC/IFRC standards. Interview with Representatives in 2020 Safety No known safety hazards are related to this product. Complementary Technical Systems None Academic Research and References “Target region,” Nrsrelief.com. Available: https://www.nrsrelief.com/ N. R. S. Relief, “Our impact,” Nrsrelief.com, 16-May-2016. Available: https://www.nrsrelief.com/about-us/our-impact/ “Goal 6,” Sdgs.un.org. Available: https://sdgs.un.org/goals/goal6 “Aquatainer 10L Collapsible,” Nrsrelief.com. Available: https://www.nrsrelief.com/products/aquatainer-10-collapsible/ “Aquatainer 10L Collapsible,” Engineering For Change. Available: http://old.engineeringforchange.org/solutions/product/aquatainer-10l-collapsible/ “Contact manufacturer,” Nrsrelief.com. Available: https://www.nrsrelief.com/ouroffice/ “Embracing lean production to better serve vulnerable populations,” Nrsrelief.com. Available: https://www.nrsrelief.com/blog/embracing-lean-production-to-better-serve-vulnerable-populations/ Compliance with regulations Manufactured from food grade materials without toxic elements according to EN 1186-3-9 standard. NRS Relief products comply with ISO standards: ISO 14000: 2004 Environmental Management ISO 9001: 2015 Quality Management, Sales, Business Development, Supply Chain Management, Warehousing, Relief Supplies SA 8000 - Certification - Social Accountability Evaluation methods The product is evaluated for impact resistance through impact/drop tests by the manufacturer. Other Information None Comments from the Community 5 Comments Alex says: October 11, 2016 at 1:02 am In addition, the adherence to the USAID endorsed design principles is well noted. E4C.COMMENTS says: December 12, 2016 at 5:29 am Noel Wilson says: Specs, schematics and Tech support section is great. Noel Wilson says: December 12, 2016 at 5:26 pm The ‘This keeps children learning’ section could be removed as it is a big assumption. E4C.COMMENTS says: March 2, 2017 at 6:24 am Anna Murray says: This safe storage container has an integrated tap, handle for carrying, and is the same typical size as most jerry cans used for drinking water in sub-Saharan Africa. It is also manufactured in several countries, which supports local economies and keeps shipping costs lower. While the small opening is advantageous to prevent users from dipping hands or cups into the container, it also makes it difficult to clean inside. Overall it seems like a well-designed safe storage container. Anna Murray says: March 24, 2017 at 3:09 am – The “Competitive landscape” entry here is not the same as the other two water containers, which may have only listed jerrycans. – “The Aquatainer improves access to clean water for low-income populations.” This is a bit of a bold statement. A container doesn’t really directly improve access to clean water. It helps maintain the quality of water during transport and storage. Leave a Reply You must be logged in to post a comment.
